html:
Projection to a vanishing point
Projection lines pass through O and intersect the camera plane. When VZ ≠ 0, all image positions converge to p∞.
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
$$\begin{aligned}
x(t) &= f \frac{X + V_X t}{Z + V_Z t}\\
y(t) &= f \frac{Y + V_Y t}{Z + V_Z t} 
\end{aligned}$$ 

If $\dot{Z}=0$, then the projected point will move with
constant velocity over time, as shown in equation (@eq-parallelmotion).
If $\dot{Z} \neq  0$, then, as time goes to infinity, the point will
converge to a **vanishing point**: 

$$\begin{aligned}
\lim_{t \to \infty} x(t)  &= f \frac{V_X}{V_Z} = x_{\infty}\\
\lim_{t \to \infty} y(t) &= f \frac{V_Y}{V_Z} = y_{\infty}
\end{aligned}$${#eq-motionvanishingpoint}

The following sketch (@fig-flying_bird) shows Gibson's bird (see
@fig-gibson_bird) flying away from the camera along a straight line. In
the camera the bird gets smaller as it flies away until it disappears at
the vanishing point. In this drawing the vanishing point is within the
view of the camera.

![Projection onto the camera plane of the sequence produced by a bird flying away. The bird will vanish at the **vanishing point**](figures/optical_flow/flying_bird.png){width="100%" #fig-flying_bird}

The vanishing point is the location
$\mathbf{p}_{\infty}=(x_{\infty}, y_{\infty})^\mathsf{T}$ where the
moving point slowly converges to. The location of the vanishing point is
independent of the point location at time $t=0$, and it only depends on
the 3D velocity vector, $\mathbf{V}$. Therefore, if the scene contains
multiple points at different locations moving with the same velocity,
they will converge to the same vanishing point.

### Camera Translation

Let's now assume that the scene is static and that only the camera is
moving. In this case, all the observed motion in the image will be due
to the motion of the camera.

interactions.label: Bird Velocity
interactions.teaches: how the speed of the bird affects its projection size and motion towards the vanishing point

interactions.label: Camera Motion
interactions.teaches: how camera movement impacts the perception of motion in the scene

interactions.label: Z Velocity
interactions.teaches: the influence of Z-axis velocity on when or if the bird reaches the vanishing point

demo_steps.title: Initial Bird Movement
demo_steps.narration: Watch how the bird moves away from the camera. Notice that it gradually becomes smaller as it approaches the vanishing point.

demo_steps.title: Approaching the Vanishing Point
demo_steps.narration: As the bird continues along its path, it shrinks and appears to move slower across the screen due to the vanishing point.

demo_steps.title: Effect of Camera Motion
demo_steps.narration: Now let's introduce camera motion. Notice how the bird's motion is affected as the camera starts moving as well.

demo_steps.title: Convergence to Vanishing Point
demo_steps.narration: With increased Z velocity, observe how the bird converges more rapidly to its vanishing point, disappearing from view.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: An oblique viewpoint from behind the initial camera position, zoomed out to include the vanishing point clearly in the frame.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure smooth transitions for changes in velocity and camera motion. Consider visual cues like motion blur for the bird at higher velocities.

discrepancies: Red arrows in the generated figure are less prominent.
Birds in the generated figure appear less detailed.
Labels in the generated figure are slightly misplaced and smaller.
Color intensity for green lines is lower in the generated version.
Vanishing point label formatting differs slightly.
evaluation.notes: The generated figure captures the basic idea but lacks in label clarity and color accuracy.
action_items: Align and scale labels to match the original image more closely.
Increase the prominence and color intensity of the red arrows.
Improve the detail of the bird illustrations for better recognition.

feedback.rationale: The generated figure mostly needs execution improvements in label placement, color intensity, and detail enhancement.
action_items: Align and scale labels to match the original image more closely.

Improve the detail of the bird illustrations for better recognition.

attempts.html:
Projection and vanishing point
Positive Z velocity makes projected birds shrink and converge to p∞.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Camera plane label misaligned with the plane
Vanishing point text smaller in generation
Birds not as clearly outlined
Arrows slightly thinner in generation
Missing the green lines connecting birds
evaluation.notes: Interactive elements are useful but geometric alignment and interpretation are flawed, affecting conceptual clarity.
action_items: Align labels accurately with corresponding features in the 3D scene.
Correct the colors and thickness of arrows and lines to match the source image.

feedback.rationale: The errors primarily involve misalignment, label sizing, color, and thickness issues that point to execution problems in the generation rather than a fundamental planning issue.
action_items: Align labels accurately with corresponding features in the 3D scene.
Correct the colors and thickness of arrows and lines to match the source image.

Transparency on certain elements alters visual clarity.
evaluation.notes: Good interactivity and reasonable concept fidelity, but visual discrepancies affect overall appearance.

Standardize the spacing and length of red arrows.
Uniformly display green lines for consistency and clarity.
